key_insight: Large AI models can turn solid electrolyte discovery from slow, intuition-led screening into a closed-loop, self-improving pipeline that couples data, simulation, and experiments.
engineering_relevance: This framework can accelerate the design of solid electrolytes with better ion transport, stability, and manufacturability for next-generation metal batteries.
method: Use ML interatomic potentials to extend DFT-based ion-transport simulations to longer timescales.
Use large language models to mine literature, generate hypotheses, and support scientific reasoning.
Close the loop with AI-guided candidate selection, uncertainty-aware ranking, and experimental validation.
evidence_signal: The paper is a framework and perspective rather than a single benchmark study, emphasizing the transition from static databases to dynamic, self-updating discovery systems.
watch_item: Real-world deployment will depend on standardized data, robust handling of interfacial complexity, and reproducible validation across labs.
paper_title: Breaking Bottlenecks in Solid Electrolyte Discovery with Large Artificial Intelligence Models
